assets.prompt: Quiet New England village green at dawn: birdsong, light wind through spring grass, a dog barking far away, a distant military drum. No music, no voices.
Quiet 18th century interior room tone: muffled street sounds through shuttered windows, occasional flies buzzing, a wooden chair creak, a single quill scratching on paper. No music, no voices.
Night river crossing in a winter storm: wind and driving sleet, chunks of ice grinding and knocking against a wooden hull, oars creaking in oarlocks, water lapping. No music, no voices.
A ragged volley of 18th century flintlock muskets: one sharp shot first, then dozens firing raggedly, echo across an open field, distant screams and a drum. No music.

A harbor wharf at night in winter: halyards tapping against wooden masts, water lapping on pilings, the low rustle of a large crowd standing nearly silent, one distant church bell. Sparse and quiet. No music, no clear voices.

A hatchet splitting open a large wooden tea chest: two sharp axe blows into wood, splintering planks, then a long pour of dry loose tea leaves rushing out and splashing into harbor water below. No music, no voices.

A town street in a driving sleet storm at dawn: hard sleet rattling on ice and wood, gusting wind funneled between houses, boots and iron-shod wheels crunching on frozen ruts. Cold and harsh. No music, no voices.
18th century field cannon firing down a narrow town street: two deep concussive cannon blasts in close succession, echo slapping off house fronts, debris and ice falling after. No music, no voices.

Inside a military command tent, 18th century: canvas snapping softly in wind, an oil lantern sputtering, papers shifting, distant axes chopping wood and a faraway drum. Calm and focused. No music, no voices.
A cavalry horse at full gallop through a battle: pounding hooves on turf, hard breathing, musket fire crackling in waves left and right, men shouting far off, one bugle note. No music.

A snowed-in military camp at dusk: steady bitter wind over open snow, a loose wooden door knocking, one distant axe, quiet persistent coughing from inside log huts all around. Bleak and empty. No music, no clear voices.

A siege encampment at night in light rain: rain on canvas tents, distant heavy siege cannon firing every few seconds with long rolling echoes, picks and shovels working soil, low wind. No music, no voices.
A single military snare drum beating a slow parley signal, alone, echoing over an open field, wind underneath, boots of a marching column faint in the distance. No music, no voices.

An 18th century painter's studio: a tall clock ticking, a small coal fire settling, a brush working on stretched canvas, carriage wheels passing outside on cobblestones now and then. Warm, still, interior. No music, no voices.

Repeated work opening tea chests on an 18th century merchant ship at night: rope tackle strains, a heavy wooden chest scrapes across deck planks, several measured hatchet blows split wood, loose dry tea pours over the rail into water. Sparse individual actions with quiet gaps, not a crowd spectacle. No music, no speech, no human voices.

The last tea chest work ending on an 18th century ship deck: a final short pour of loose tea into harbor water, broken wood set down, then a stiff broom sweeps tea leaves and splinters across wet deck planks. The activity resolves into quiet water and rigging. No music, no speech, no human voices.

One isolated 18th century flintlock musket shot across an open New England village green at dawn, followed only by its natural echo and a brief return to still air. Exactly one shot: no volley, no second gun, no screams, no drum, no music, no speech, no human voices.

Two draft horses standing inside a broad wooden Durham boat at night: hooves shift and stamp carefully on wet timber, leather harness creaks, the wooden hull flexes softly. Sparse, close, restrained animal and equipment sounds. No music, no speech, no human voices.

A heavy sheet of river ice grinds along and strikes the side of a large 18th century wooden Durham boat at night: scraping ice, two hard hull knocks, timber creaking, an oarlock shuddering, black water sloshing. No music, no speech, no human voices.

A nor'easter intensifying over a wide winter river at night: wind rises steadily, hard sleet drives across open water, choppy waves slap a wooden boat, loose ice grinds nearby. Strong but with space in the midrange for documentary narration. No thunder, no music, no speech, no human voices.

A restrained night assault on an 18th century earthen redoubt in light rain: hurried boots in mud, soldiers climbing timber obstacles, wood splintering, metal bayonets striking wood and equipment, then the activity subsides. Distant and non-graphic. No muskets, no gunshots, no explosions, no music, no speech, no human voices.

scenes.label: The Tea Party
scenes.musicPolicy: No music during the wharf, interaction, or Parliament handoff.

cues.spatial: Non-spatial stereo bed; rigging and bell remain distant.
cues.caveat: The crowd must remain indistinct; no invented chants or legible speech.

cues.spatial: Close at the worked chest and rail.
cues.caveat: Tea was split and poured; do not imply theft or injury.

cues.spatial: Near-field work shifts among chests; never a wall of impacts.
cues.caveat: Repeated work is supported generally, but exact tool rhythm is interpretive.

cues.spatial: At the final chest, then broad deck sweep.
cues.caveat: The deck sweeping is recorded; the precise last-chest sequence is staged.

cues.spatial: All interaction events stop; only the sparse wharf bed remains.
cues.caveat: This silence protects Parliament narration. Chest-work is latched off at chests-done and the completion take ends naturally before TEA-080; generate no filler.

scenes.label: Lexington Green
scenes.musicPolicy: The isolated shot, volley, and aftermath are unscored.

cues.spatial: Open stereo field; drum remains far beyond the green.
cues.caveat: No battle activity before the disputed first shot.

cues.spatial: Distant and deliberately unlocalized across the green.
cues.caveat: The source and position of the first shot are unknown; never localize a shooter.

cues.spatial: Baked into the volley edit; field-width, not close gore.
cues.caveat: Combined baseline file is only the volley sequence, never the isolated first shot.

cues.spatial: Full-field stillness before aftermath narration.
cues.caveat: Four-second hold is a timing requirement for #45; generate no aftermath filler.

scenes.label: The Declaration
scenes.musicPolicy: No music under narration or during the signature hold.

cues.spatial: Quiet room bed; quill remains across the room, not at the viewer's hand.
cues.caveat: The exact room texture is interpretive; no legible street speech.

cues.spatial: Room tone falls away while ink dries.
cues.caveat: Issue #3 owns Declaration files; this separate mapping is the exact integration handoff.

scenes.label: Crossing the Delaware
scenes.musicPolicy: No music; storm layers must leave narration intelligible.

cues.spatial: Wide river bed; hull and oarlocks near, wind broad.
cues.caveat: Leutze's later image is not an audio reference; keep the crossing dark and workmanlike.

cues.spatial: From a neighboring cargo boat, never beside the viewer's ear.
cues.caveat: Horses and artillery crossed in separate boats; do not place them in every boat.

cues.spatial: At the hull side, alternating left/right is optional in final mix.
cues.caveat: Ice contact is documented generally; exact impacts are staged for interaction feedback.

cues.spatial: Wide layer above the base river bed; keep center midrange open.
cues.caveat: Weather accounts support rain, sleet, and snow; no thunder is asserted.

scenes.label: Trenton
scenes.musicPolicy: No music until the separately owned final cue; battle remains unscored.

cues.spatial: Street-width wind with close boots and wheels.
cues.caveat: Keep formations indistinct; ambience must not invent shouted commands.

cues.spatial: Ahead in the street, reflections from building fronts.
cues.caveat: Two representative reports, not a literal count of Knox's battery.

cues.spatial: Cannon and event sounds end; sleet bed remains.
cues.caveat: Do not add victory cheers.

scenes.label: Saratoga
scenes.musicPolicy: No score during the charge burst.

cues.spatial: Enclosed canvas room, work sounds far outside.
cues.caveat: Far drum is texture, not a specific documented signal.

cues.spatial: Viewer-centered hoof movement; battle remains around, not inside the head.
cues.caveat: Bugle and shout texture are interpretive and must remain indistinct.

scenes.label: Valley Forge
scenes.musicPolicy: No music anywhere in the scene.

cues.spatial: Broad wind; door, axe, and coughs remain separated and distant.
cues.caveat: Coughs are non-verbal texture, not cast dialogue; drill cadence remains VO-owned.

cues.spatial: No added event or transition sound; wind alone holds the dusk.
cues.caveat: Purposeful restraint; generate no emotional sweetener.

scenes.label: Yorktown
scenes.musicPolicy: No score in siege or assault; source tune remains issue #18. No musket reports in Redoubt 10.

cues.spatial: Rain and trench work close; siege cannon very distant.
cues.caveat: Cannon belongs to siege context, not the unloaded-musket assault foreground.

cues.spatial: Close movement and obstacles; impacts remain restrained and non-graphic.
cues.caveat: Strict invariant: unloaded muskets, no gunshots or explosions in this file.

cues.spatial: Begins at the parapet, then recedes toward the surrender field.
cues.caveat: A parley drum is supported; exact rhythm and marching distance are interpretive.

scenes.label: Treaty of Paris
scenes.musicPolicy: No music in the three factual void dwells or the signature payoff.

cues.spatial: Interior stereo bed; carriage remains beyond the wall.
cues.caveat: The studio is reconstructed; clock, fire, brush, and carriage imply place, not a documented recording.

cues.spatial: Near silence before and throughout the aged-signature hold.
cues.caveat: No generated payoff sound; the signature remains wordless.
